# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates an IAM role for Lambda functions. Grant the role permissions to access S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, and CloudWatch. Use wildcard actions (*) for flexibility as we add new features. This role is used across multiple Lambda functions.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

Resources:
  IAMRoleLambdaExecution:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-lambdaexecution-role' # Not recommended as this name will change each time you deploy, e.g., prodstack_prodaccount1234567890abcdefghi
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument: 
        Version: "2012-10-17"  
        Statement:    # Example policy for a service that can use this role and to assume it. Replace as necessary with your needs
          Action:    
            - sts:AssumeRole              
          Principal: 
             Service: lambda.amazonaws.com      
      Policies:  
        - PolicyName: RootPolicy # IAM policy created from the example statement above, then we can allow other actions to use this role by adding them below like so... (Note that you should delete these policies and statements as per your project requirements) 
          PolicyDocumentId: !Sub '${AWS::UUID}'   // This is a unique identifier for each policy document. It will change if the resource or properties are changed, e.g., If IAM Role updated to use another service that has permission then you would need replace this value with new Permission set here...
          PolicyStatements:     # Example of what statements allow all actions in a role for lambda function usage (replace as necessary...) 
            - Effect: "Allow"  
              Action : '*'
